queue.title: The math behind one faster estimate.
queue.content:
Tree company math:

Average job value: $1,800
Lead response time (manual): 24-48 hours
Close rate on fast response (under 2 hours): ~45%
Close rate on slow response (24+ hours): ~12%

If you're getting 20 web leads a month:
- 20 leads × 45% close = 9 jobs
- 20 leads × 12% close = 2.4 jobs

That's 6.6 extra jobs per month from the same leads you're already getting.
6.6 × $1,800 = $11,880 in additional monthly revenue.

Cost of TreeSnap: $79/month.

The leads are already there. You just need to respond faster.

queue.title: What does the AI actually see in the photos?
queue.content:
Fair question. Here's what TreeSnap's AI looks for in customer photos:

Tree size and height (diameter, canopy spread)
Proximity to structures (house, fence, power lines)
Condition (dead, diseased, storm-damaged, healthy)
Access constraints (yard gates, slope, overhead lines)
Work type (removal, trimming, deadwood, stump)

From those factors, it builds a line-item estimate using real market rates for your region.

You review the estimate before it goes anywhere. You adjust it if you need to. You call the customer with a number that already makes sense.

It's not magic. It's pattern recognition trained on thousands of tree jobs.

https://treesnap.cloud

#TreeService #AI #TreeCare #ArboristLife #Technology #SmallBusiness

queue.title: How long does a manual tree estimate actually take you?
queue.content:
Be honest: how much time are you spending on estimates every week?

Drive to the site: 20-45 min round trip
Walk the property, measure, assess: 20-30 min
Write up the estimate back at the truck or at home: 15-20 min
Send it, follow up: 10 min

That's 65-105 minutes per estimate. For every lead. Including the ones who don't book.

If you're running 15 estimates a month, that's up to 26 hours. That's over half a work week just on bids.

TreeSnap does the estimate in 2 minutes. You spend your time on jobs that close.

Start your trial at https://treesnap.cloud

#TreeService #TreeRemoval #ArboristLife #TimeIsMoney #LocalBusiness #SmallBusiness

queue.title: What separates a $600 estimate from a $2,200 estimate for the same tree?
queue.content:
Customers don't know what tree work costs. That's the point.

When they get three wildly different quotes, they don't know which one is fair. They default to the one that seemed most professional, or the one who responded first, or the one who broke down exactly what they're paying for.

A vague number loses to an itemized estimate almost every time.

TreeSnap builds a line-item estimate from the photos: removal, stump grinding, debris haul, access difficulty, crane if needed. Every charge has a reason.

When you call with a clear, broken-down number, you're not just quoting. You're educating. And that wins jobs.
